title: 10 Best Medicinal Preparations of Chamomilla recutita (German Chamomile)

preparations.constituent_desc: Chamomilla Recutita infusion is a medicinal preparation made by steeping dried flowers of German Chamomile in hot water, which releases the medicinal compounds found in the plant. The main health benefits of this preparation include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote sleep and relaxation. The flowers of the Chamomilla Recutita plant are used to make this infusion. To make the infusion, pour boiling water over 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes before straining. Some precautions to take when using this preparation include avoiding it if allergic to chamomile or other members of the daisy family, and being cautious when using it in large amounts or for extended periods, as it can cause side effects such as vomiting and skin rashes.

Chamomilla Recutita tincture is a medicinal preparation made by soaking dried German Chamomile flowers in a mixture of water and alcohol for several weeks to extract the medicinal compounds, which includes various terpenoids and flavonoids. The main health benefits of this Chamomilla Recutita preparation include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate anxiety and insomnia due to its calming effects on the nervous system. The main plant parts used for this Chamomilla Recutita preparation are the dried flowers of the German Chamomile plant. To make Chamomilla Recutita tincture, dried German Chamomile flowers are soaked in a mixture of water and alcohol, typically at a ratio of 1:5 parts of herb to menstruum, and left to steep for 2-6 weeks before being pressed and filtered. When using Chamomilla Recutita tincture, it is important to follow the recommended dosage as high concentrations can have a sedative effect and may interact with other medications. Additionally, individuals who are allergic to members of the daisy family or have a history of allergic reactions to plants should exercise caution when using this tincture. It is also recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using Chamomilla Recutita tincture, especially for pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with medical conditions or taking other medications.

Chamomilla Recutita fluid extract is a concentrated liquid form of German Chamomile, a medicinal herb, produced by extracting the active constituents of the plant using a solvent such as alcohol or glycerin. Its main health benefits include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic properties, making it a popular remedy for digestive complaints such as indigestion, colic, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as for relieving anxiety and promoting sleep. The flower heads are the primary plant parts used for this preparation, which is made by macerating the dried flowers in a solvent, then filtering and preserving the resulting liquid. When using this Chamomilla Recutita preparation, it is important to follow the recommended dosage, as excessive consumption may cause allergic reactions, vomiting, or skin rashes. Additionally, people who are allergic to ragweed or other members of the Asteraceae family should use Chamomilla Recutita fluid extract with caution, as they may have cross-reactivity to this herb. Pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult a healthcare professional before using this preparation, as its safety during pregnancy and lactation has not been established.

Chamomilla Recutita dry extract is a concentrated form of the German Chamomile plant, created by removing the water and solvent from the chamomile flowers through a drying process. Its main health benefits include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues such as indigestion, gas, and stomach cramps, and promoting sleep and relaxation due to its calming properties. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the daisy-like flowers of the German Chamomile plant. To make the dry extract, the flowers are harvested, dried, and then undergo an extraction process using a solvent, which is then evaporated to leave behind a dry, powdered extract. When using Chamomilla Recutita dry extract, it is important to follow the recommended dosage, as excessive use may cause side effects such as vomiting and skin reactions. Additionally, people who are allergic to members of the daisy family should avoid using this preparation, as they may have an allergic reaction. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should consult with a healthcare provider before using this preparation, as its safety during pregnancy and lactation has not been established.

Chamomilla Recutita capsules are dietary supplements that contain dried and ground flowers of German Chamomile, a plant native to Europe and parts of Asia and now grown in many countries around the world. The main health benefits of this preparation include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote sleep. The flowers and occasionally the leaves of the Chamomilla recutita plant are used for medicinal purposes. To make Chamomilla recutita capsules, the flowers are harvested, dried, and then ground into a fine powder, which is then encapsulated. When taking Chamomilla recutita capsules, it is important to follow the recommended dosage on the packaging, as taking too much may result in side effects such as nausea, vomiting, and skin reactions. Additionally, people who are allergic to ragweed, daisies, or other members of the Asteraceae family should use chamomile products with caution, as they may cause an allergic reaction.

Chamomilla Recutita compress is a medicinal preparation that involves soaking the flowers of German Chamomile in hot water and then applying the cooled liquid or a cloth soaked in it to the skin. The main health benefits of this preparation include its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various skin conditions such as rashes, eczema, and minor burns. The flowers of the German Chamomile plant are the main plant parts used for this preparation. To make a Chamomilla Recutita compress, first, boil water and pour it over dried chamomile flowers. Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain out the flowers. Soak a clean cloth in the cooled liquid and apply it to the affected area for 10-15 minutes, several times a day. When using a Chamomilla Recutita compress, it is important to be aware that some people may experience allergic reactions, especially if they have a known allergy to ragweed or other members of the Asteraceae family. Discontinue use if you notice any signs of an allergic reaction, such as itching, redness, or swelling. Additionally, it is recommended to do a patch test before applying the compress to a large area of skin.

Chamomilla Recutita ointment is a medicinal preparation made from the flowers of the German Chamomile plant, a species native to Europe and parts of Asia and North America. Its main health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties, making it effective in treating various skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and rashes, as well as reducing pain and inflammation in muscle and joint aches. The flowers are the primary plant part used in this preparation, which contain therapeutic compounds like azulene, flavonoids, and terpenoids. To make Chamomilla Recutita ointment, the dried or fresh flowers are steeped in a carrier oil, such as olive or almond oil, and then combined with beeswax or another natural wax to thicken the mixture. Once cooled, the ointment can be applied topically to the affected area. However, it is essential to take precautions when using Chamomilla Recutita ointment, as it can cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with a known allergy to ragweed or other members of the Asteraceae family. It is also recommended to avoid using this ointment on open wounds or broken skin, and to discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur.

Chamomilla Recutita liniment is a medicinal preparation that is made from the flowers and leaves of the German Chamomile plant, also known as Chamomilla recutita. This liniment is known for its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and calming properties, making it a popular choice for relieving muscle and joint pain, strains, and sprains. To make Chamomilla Recutita liniment, the flowers and leaves of the German Chamomile plant are steeped in a base oil, such as olive or almond oil, for several weeks. The resulting infused oil is then strained and mixed with a small amount of rubbing alcohol or witch hazel to preserve it. When using Chamomilla Recutita liniment, it is important to avoid contact with the eyes and open wounds, as it can cause irritation. Additionally, it is recommended to do a patch test on a small area of skin before using it more broadly, as some people may have an allergic reaction to the preparation.

Chamomilla Recutita oil, also known as German Chamomile oil, is a popular medicinal preparation derived from the flowers of the Chamomilla recutita plant. Its main health benefits include its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and calming properties, making it a popular choice for treating conditions such as muscle aches, skin irritations, and insomnia. The flowers and leaves of the Chamomilla recutita plant are used to produce this oil through a steam distillation process. To make Chamomilla Recutita oil, the flowers and leaves are harvested, dried, and then steam-distilled to extract the essential oil. When using Chamomilla Recutita oil, it is important to take certain precautions, such as diluting it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin, as it can cause skin irritation if used in its pure form. Additionally, it is recommended to perform a patch test before using it on larger areas of the skin, and to avoid using it if pregnant or breastfeeding.

Chamomilla Recutita tea is an herbal infusion made from the flowers of the German Chamomile plant, a member of the daisy family. Its main health benefits include promoting sleep and relaxation, soothing digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and gas, and providing anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate menstrual cramps and skin irritations. The flowers are the primary plant part used for this preparation. To make Chamomilla Recutita tea, pour boiling water over dried chamomile flowers and let them steep for 5-10 minutes. Some precautions to take when using this Chamomilla Recutita preparation include avoiding it if pregnant or breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions or cause allergic reactions in some individuals. Additionally, those taking blood-thinning medications should consult with a healthcare provider before consuming chamomile tea, as it may interact with these medications.

intro: Chamomile, with the botanical name Chamomilla recutita, is a popular medicinal herb that has been used for centuries to treat a variety of ailments. Its flowers and leaves are used to make medicinal preparations such as tisanes (herbal teas) and tinctures. A tisane made from chamomile is often used to help promote sleep and relaxation, and it may also have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Tinctures, which are alcohol-based extracts of the plant, can be used to treat a variety of issues including digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations. Chamomile is generally considered safe, but it can cause allergic reactions in some people, particularly those with allergies to other members of the daisy family. It is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
